description: Obscura obstacle course: a feature + speed showcase. Stages span frameworks (React/Preact/Vue), capability (async render, SPA routing, timers), the web-API surface (URL/TextDecoder/FileAPI/Range/Selection/custom elements/dialog/input), extraction (--dump text/markdown/links/html), and scraping/stealth (fingerprint/cookies/charset decoding). Each asserts the JS-rendered DOM or CLI output matches an expected value and records per-page latency; the runner exits non-zero on any mismatch.

stages.name: static
stages.desc: baseline: HTML parse + querySelectorAll

stages.name: dom-build
stages.desc: vanilla-JS DOM throughput (build 5000 rows)

stages.name: react
stages.desc: client-side React render (the Lightpanda-can't differentiator)

stages.name: preact
stages.desc: client-side Preact render via h()/render() (100-item list)

stages.name: vue
stages.desc: client-side Vue 3 render via createApp/mount (100-item list)

stages.name: async-render
stages.desc: fetch() same-origin JSON then render (async settle)

stages.name: spa-router
stages.desc: pushState client-side routing

stages.name: timers
stages.desc: setTimeout chain + microtask (event-loop settle)

stages.name: web-component
stages.desc: custom element + shadow DOM render

stages.name: url
stages.desc: WHATWG URL parser: decompose https://u:p@h.test:8/a?x=1#y into protocol/username/password/hostname/port/host/pathname/search/hash

stages.name: textdecoder
stages.desc: TextDecoder legacy charset: new TextDecoder('shift_jis').decode([0x82,0xA0]) yields the Japanese hiragana あ via the Encoding Standard index tables

stages.name: fileapi
stages.desc: File API: Blob construction (size 18) plus async FileReader.readAsText load event returning the concatenated blob text

stages.name: range
stages.desc: Range: createRange + selectNodeContents over a 3-child container, toString walks the subtree (offsets 0..3)

stages.name: selection
stages.desc: Selection: getSelection().selectAllChildren produces one range and toString returns the container's text

stages.name: custom-element
stages.desc: Custom element: customElements.define upgrades the connected element and connectedCallback sets its content

stages.name: dialog
stages.desc: HTMLDialogElement: showModal sets open=true, close('confirmed') clears open and sets returnValue

stages.name: input-step
stages.desc: HTMLInputElement number: valueAsNumber reads 10, stepUp(3) advances by 3*step(5) to 25 and reflects into value

stages.name: extract-article
expect_contains: Obscura runs real JavaScript so the extracted DOM matches what a user would see.
Readability extraction keeps the main article body and discards surrounding chrome.
stages.desc: --dump text readability extraction: keeps the <article> body, strips <nav>/<header>/<aside>/<footer> boilerplate

stages.name: extract-markdown
stages.desc: --dump markdown: headings (# / ##), list bullets (- ), link syntax ](url), and fenced code blocks

stages.name: extract-links
stages.desc: --dump links: harvests every anchor href from the rendered page

stages.name: extract-html
stages.desc: --dump html: serializes the live DOM including a JS-injected element to prove rendered state is captured

stages.name: fingerprint
stages.desc: stealth fingerprint: navigator.userAgent reads as Chrome, navigator.webdriver is not advertised (undefined/false), and platform/language/timezone are populated and consistent

stages.name: cookies
stages.desc: document.cookie round-trip: cookies written from JS are read back from a real cookie jar

stages.name: charset-shiftjis
stages.desc: charset meta-sniff: page stored as raw Shift_JIS bytes with <meta charset=shift_jis>; obscura decodes the body to correct Japanese text
